content.claim: Among early school leavers in Hungary, the most vulnerable groups are those living in rural areas, Roma, and people with disabilities; in 2023, 58.7% of Roma students aged 18-24 were school dropouts compared with 9.3% among non-Roma, and the share of disadvantaged students reaching a good level in basic skills is 12.1%, considerably below the EU average of 16.3%.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: These are system-wide outcome gaps not attributable specifically to the 6/8-year gimnázium mechanism; early leaving concentrates mainly in vocational tracks rather than gimnázium non-admission specifically
The data describe compositional sorting outcomes rather than isolate the causal effect of early academic selection from the broader tracked secondary system
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
